steps.title: Identify what the question asks
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that most effectively emphasizes a difference between Vickers and Crosfield. The notes contrast them: Vickers studied algae in the Canary Islands; Crosfield studied the hills of Northeast Wales. The correct choice must directly contrast these two scientists — their fields or locations must appear side by side with a contrast marker.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Though it was conducted long ago, the research performed by algologist Anna Vickers and geologist Margaret Crosfield can help us understand how scientific knowledge evolves.’: Groups both scientists together to make a point about historical research — no contrast between them is drawn. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Algology and geology are just two examples of the myriad fields in which scientists can specialize.’: Makes a general point about scientific fields without mentioning either scientist by name. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Anna Vickers studied algae in the Canary Islands; Margaret Crosfield, however, studied the hills of Northeast Wales.’: Uses a semicolon and “however” to directly contrast what each scientist studied — exactly what the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Both Anna Vickers and Margaret Crosfield are known for their contributions to science.’: Emphasizes similarity (“Both”), not a difference. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
